summary.most_likely_root_cause: duplicate launcher/process topology, with DB unique enforcement absent, failed, or not active in the live DB at the time duplicates were written

pre_containment_process_topology: launcher pid 10920 -> launcher pid 38984 -> child stack {recommendation_api 37232, bot_core 40320, dashboard_server 17528, resolution_watcher 6852}
separate stack under parent pid 29228 -> {recommendation_api 26064, bot_core 42956, dashboard_server 42808, resolution_watcher 10516}
duplicate_open_live_evidence: paired BRIDGE OPEN log lines for ids 111 and 112 on mlb-hou-oak-2026-04-05
capacity_breach_evidence: Repeated 'BRIDGE SKIP - at capacity (4/3)' in bot_baseball_20260405.log
code_path_evidence: bot_core.py bridge path calls insert_open_trade(trade, sport=SPORT)
core/db.py live file contains BEGIN IMMEDIATE pre-check and int | None return path
db_path_evidence: No visible DB_PATH override was confirmed from inspected .env output slice
evidence.index_check: Could not directly query sqlite indexes because sqlite3 CLI is not installed and inline Python DB inspection is blocked by approval/obfuscation policy in this channel

conclusions: Duplicate processes were real and were contributing directly to duplicate opens.
Safe containment succeeded by removing one duplicate launcher tree.
Active post-containment stack is now a single valid process topology.
Open count remains 4 because duplicate open rows already exist in backend state/DB from before containment.
The bridge path does call insert_open_trade() in live code, so the remaining unanswered question is why the live DB did not prevent the duplicate write pair.
blocked_items: Live DB unique index presence could not be directly verified with available non-obfuscated tooling in this channel.
next_action_needed: Manager should issue either (a) a narrow DB-inspection task with approved tooling to confirm the live index/schema state, and/or (b) a cleanup/remediation task for existing duplicate open rows now that duplicate process topology has been contained.
